data.title: Application for deleting the admission to trading of the company's shares from Nasdaq First North Growth Market Denmark
data.description:
Company Announcement no. 137/2026 (January 22, 2026)

Application for deleting the admission to trading of the company's shares from the trading platform Nasdaq First North Growth Market Denmark has been submitted.

LED iBond International A/S hereby announces that, following the decision made at the Extraordinary General Meeting held on 16 January 2026, the Company has today submitted an application to Nasdaq for deleting the admission to trading of the company's shares from the trading platform Nasdaq First North Growth Market Denmark.

Further information will be provided once Nasdaq First North Growth Market Denmark has processed the application.

Further Information

Michael Brag, Chairman

Mobile: +45 25 10 12 00

Email:mbr@ledibond.com

Company Contact

LED iBond International A/S

Ryttermarken 10, 1.

3520 Farum

Denmark

CVR 36041609

+45 7070 7855

info@ledibond.com

www.ledibond.com

Certified advisor

HC Andersen Capital

Bredgade 23B, 2.

1260 København K

Denmark

Telephone: +45 30 93 18 87

Email:ca@hcandersencapital.dk

About LED iBond International A/S

Founded in 2014, LED iBond offers sustainable, innovative lighting solutions, based on years of development and deep knowledge of modern LED technology combined with advanced packaging and simple assembly technology.

LED iBond is focusing on three key business lines: Parking Facilities (Parking Houses & Solar Carports), Indoor Farming (Greenhouses & Vertical farms) and Service Stations (Canopies & Car wash).

Our technology platforms TRACY®, GRACY™ and HORTISABER™ offer unique value propositions.

TRACY® and GRACY™ are developed to meet demands for high design flexibility, robustness, easy integration into building construction, less cables and low total costs of ownership due to market leading energy efficiency.

HORTISABER™ is a new generation of LED grow lights for indoor farming. It’s developed to meet the demands from modern greenhouse productions with outstanding energy efficiency, light uniformity, superior passive cooling design that enhances the lifespan significantly and the unique linear slim-line design ensures minimal daylight shadow.

LED iBond’s lighting solutions have been installed in many large-scale projects ranging from Parking Houses, Charging Hubs, Solar Carports and Greenhouses to customised illumination projects such as shopping centres, intelligent shelf lighting and elevator ceilings.

LED iBond is a Danish design and manufacturing company.

LED iBond International A/S is admitted to trading on Nasdaq First North Growth Market Denmark

(ticker: LEDIBOND).

Attachments

Application for deleting the admission to trading of the company's shares from Nasdaq First North Growth Market Denmark

data.title: US Stocks Post Strong Gains
data.description:
US stocks extended gains on Thursday, with both the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq adding 0.8%, and the Dow Jones soaring almost 500 points.

The positive momentum followed President Trump’s announcement that he would suspend plans to impose new tariffs on a group of European countries, citing a “framework agreement” over Greenland reached after a meeting with the NATO Secretary General.

News that Alibaba Group is preparing to list its chipmaking unit and by comments from Nvidia’s CEO also lifted investors' mood.

Mega-cap stocks traded higher, including Nvidia (+0.5%), Microsoft (+1.1%), Apple (+0.9%), Amazon (+0.7%), Meta (+3%), Alphabet (+1.6%), Broadcom (+1.1%), and Tesla (+0.5%).

Sentiment was further supported by solid US economic data, with Q3 GDP growth revised up to 4.4% from 4.3% and weekly jobless claims falling to 200K. Meanwhile, General Electric slid 5.7% despite beating earnings expectations, while P&G added 2.3% despite reporting a revenue miss.

data.title: ACNB Corp slightly beats Q4 net income  estimates
data.description:
Overview

  • Financial holding company's Q4 net income slightly beat analyst estimates, EPS missed expectations

  • Company's Q4 results impacted by Traditions Bancorp acquisition and securities portfolio repositioning

  • Net interest income for Q4 increased significantly yr/yr, driven by acquisition

Outlook

  • ACNB did not provide specific financial guidance for 2026

Result Drivers

  • ACQUISITION IMPACT - The acquisition of Traditions Bancorp drove increases in average loan balances and net interest income

  • SECURITIES REPOSITIONING - Repositioning of investment securities portfolio led to a $3.6 mln pre-tax loss, affecting noninterest income

  • NET INTEREST MARGIN - Higher asset yields and lower deposit costs improved net interest margin to 4.36% in Q4

Key Details

Metric

Beat/Miss

Actual

*Applies to a deviation of less than 1%; not applicable for per-share numbers.

Analyst Coverage

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 3 "strong buy" or "buy", 1 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the banks peer group is "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for ACNB Corp is $56.00, about 10.4% above its January 21 closing price of $50.71

  • The stock recently traded at 10 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 9 three months ago

Press Release:

data.title: Trump's About-Face on Greenland Thrusts Europe Into Another Dilemma — WSJ
data.description:
By Laurence Norman and Kim Mackrael

BRUSSELS — After President Trump's reversal on Greenland on Wednesday, European leaders no longer need to draft trade-warfare plans against their biggest economic partner.

Instead, when they meet here Thursday evening, they will tackle a far thornier question: how to manage an unpredictable U.S. administration that scorns and threatens its NATO allies?

The conundrum has hung over European leaders since Trump returned to the White House. For a year, they employed flattery and deference to seek compromise. When Trump's ire focused on them, they grinned and bore it.

But over the past week, something fundamental broke in trans-Atlantic relations, say politicians and diplomats across Europe. Even leaders who have sought to avoid any open confrontation with Trump, like Britain's Keir Starmer, started pushing back.

Washington's threats against North Atlantic Treaty Organization ally Denmark and its backers, as well as Trump's attacks on Europe, left leaders across the continent wondering if their once-closest ally had crossed the line into becoming an outright threat. Even after Trump's about-face, Europeans remain wary and regard the relationship as critically damaged.

The central question European Union leaders now face is whether they can muddle through to the end of Trump's term by swerving between compromise and retaliation, or whether Europe can build some genuine independence that would require reduced defense dependency on Washington, stronger economies and diversified export markets.

Other NATO allies face similar dilemmas.

Mark Carney, the prime minister of Canada, a country that Trump has said should become America's 51st state, offered a sharp warning at the Davos summit in Switzerland of the need to adapt to "a rupture in the world order...where the large, main-power geopolitics, is submitted to no limits, no constraints."

The U.K., whose links to the U.S. leave it particularly exposed to a rift, also stood its ground on Greenland despite Trump's threats to include it in the tariff plan.

"I will not yield. Britain will not yield on our principles and values about the future of Greenland under threats of tariffs," Starmer said in the House of Commons on Wednesday.

On Tuesday in Davos, a day before Trump's Greenland U-turn, European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen laid out a blueprint for how the EU should handle the rift in a world of growing threats and big-power politics. It was a vision that — at least in principle — many governments have now adopted.

Those blueprints involve rearmament, diversified export markets, stronger supply chains, streamlined regulation and investments in key new sectors intended to reverse years of economic decline.

"The new Europe is already emerging," von der Leyen said.

Even during the decades of full U.S. security protection, Europe was divided over how closely to cling to America. Countries including Germany, Britain and the Baltic states have long believed that hugging Washington is the only real option. Others, especially France, have argued for years that Europe must rebuild its own economic and security sovereignty.

Those divisions took a back seat over recent days, after Trump launched what looked like a looming trade war with several European governments that opposed his goal of acquiring Greenland. Trump said he would hit those countries with 10% tariffs on Feb. 1, rising to 25% in June, unless they made a deal to cede the island to the U.S.

Europeans viewed the threat as a red line requiring a much more forceful response than against the president's tariff moves last year because it challenged a country's sovereignty.

On Wednesday, the European Parliament put on hold its approval process for last summer's EU-U.S. trade deal, which Trump had touted as a U.S. triumph. EU capitals were also converging on putting retaliatory tariffs on a list of American imports worth more than $100 billion if the White House imposed its tariffs.

Member countries also weighed whether to prepare the ground to use a powerful new anticoercion trade instrument to hit back at Washington, although there were differences between France, which was fully in favor, and a more hesitant Germany.

Yet on all of these issues, European leaders are deeply aware of the continent's vulnerabilities and the risks they would face in challenging Trump. At the apex of the Greenland spat, European capitals largely avoided discussing some of the biggest weapons held in their armory to fight back against the U.S., such as slashing purchases of U.S. weapons, reconsidering U.S. bases in the region or an explicit move to tax U.S. service providers.

One important reason, say diplomats, is Ukraine. European capitals depend on U.S. arms sales and intelligence to keep Kyiv in the fight against Russia. The U.K., France and others have spent months trying to nudge Washington to provide a backstop to security guarantees they hope to offer Ukraine if the war ends. Trump hasn't publicly committed.

Meanwhile, the economic pain the EU would face from a fresh trade fight with Washington is amplified because of trade tensions with Beijing, and a European economy already in the doldrums because of high energy prices, slow growth and elevated debt levels. For some of the bloc's leading exporters, like Germany, a standoff with the U.S. could have been deeply harmful when added to reduced sales to China.

The EU has made progress in some areas meant to unleash its independence. A number of countries, including Germany, are steadily rearming against the growing threat from Russia.

Yet for now, ambitions run far ahead of reality.

The European Parliament's decision to send its just-signed trade agreement with South America for legal review served as a sharp reminder this week of just how challenging refitting the EU will be. The move could significantly delay ratification of the deal, which includes access to critical raw materials and which the two sides spent more than two decades negotiating.

The trade agreement pitted pro-free-trade Germany against a more protectionist France. French President Emmanuel Macron lobbied against the pact and all of the EU lawmakers tied to his party backed the delay.

That division once again spotlighted a painful truth for the bloc: basic political compromise to address Europe's biggest challenges eludes the bloc.

"The European Parliament's decision on the Mercosur Agreement is regrettable. It misjudges the geopolitical situation," German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said on X.

He urged EU decision makers to apply the pact provisionally while the review takes place. It is just another issue leaders are likely to clash over Thursday evening.

Write to Laurence Norman at laurence.norman@wsj.com and Kim Mackrael at kim.mackrael@wsj.com

data.title: Advanced Micro Devices Extends Seven Day Winning Streak
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (AMD, Financials) isn't slowing down. The chipmaker's stock jumped another 7.7% on Wednesday to close at $249.76, extending its winning streak to seven straight sessions. Over the past week, AMD shares have gained about 14%, even as the broader market slipped.

Investors are getting more bullish on AMD's growing presence in artificial intelligence. The company recently teamed up with Tata Consultancy Services to develop industry-specific AI and generative AI solutions a move that could open new markets for its high-performance chips.

Analysts also point to AMD's next-generation Venice EPYC processors, built on TSMC's 2 nanometer process, and its Helios AI platform as major growth drivers ahead.

Wall Street remains upbeat, with 41 analysts rating AMD a Buy and none recommending a Sell. With strong product momentum and growing AI relevance, AMD's rally may still have room to run.

data.title: PNFP: Strong 4Q25 and 2025 results with robust growth, merger integration, and positive 2026 outlook

Delivered strong 4Q25 and full-year 2025 results with double-digit growth in revenue, EPS, and tangible book value, supported by robust loan and deposit growth, successful Synovus merger integration, and healthy credit metrics. 2026 guidance targets continued growth, merger synergies, and a higher dividend.

Original document: PINNACLE FINANCIAL PARTNERS, INC [PNFP] Slides Release — Jan. 22 2026

data.title: Autodesk Cutting 1,000 Jobs As Part of Restructuring
data.description:
By Robb M. Stewart

Autodesk plans to cut about 1,000 jobs, roughly 7% of its workforce, as part of a global restructuring plan as it seeks to strengthen sales and marketing.

The move is the final phase of efforts by the maker of AutoCAD and other digital design software to streamline customer engagement and bolster its sales channels. The plan also reallocates resources in certain other functions to accelerate strategic priorities, Autodesk said Thursday.

The company said a significant portion of the job cuts will be within customer-facing sales functions.

Autodesk forecast the move will result in pretax restructuring charges of about $135 million to $160 million, largely tied to employee termination benefits. Most of the pretax restructuring charges will result in cash expenditures during the fiscal year through Jan. 31, 2027, it said. The company said it expects to record before-tax restructuring charges of roughly $90 million to $110 million in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2026, and the remainder the following year.

Autodesk said it expects to complete the plan by the end of fiscal 2027, subject to local law and consultation requirements.

Shares were up 4.1% to $268 in early trading.

Write to Robb M. Stewart at robb.stewart@wsj.com

data.title: Dow Opens Higher, Global Stocks Rise After Trump Tariff U-Turn — WSJ
data.description:
By Alexander Osipovich

Investors around the world breathed a sigh of relief after President Trump backed off his threats to impose tariffs on European countries over Greenland.

U.S. indexes rose, while European and Asian indexes also kicked higher, after Trump's U-turn triggered a relief rally Wednesday. Chip stocks, including AMD and Micron, plus other AI-related names, were among gainers in Thursday morning trading. Gold prices wavered after hitting record highs this week and oil futures dropped about 1.5%.

Risks remain. Denmark's prime minister said Thursday, "we cannot negotiate on our sovereignty," after media reports of a potential compromise involving U.S. sovereignty over land for military bases in Greenland. European Union leaders are set to discuss the territory Thursday in Brussels.

In corporate news, well-known companies reported earnings, including Procter & Gamble and GE Aerospace. P&G shares dropped after the maker of everyday goods cut its earnings outlook.

Economic data came in a little stronger than forecast. Third-quarter GDP rose 4.4% on an annualized basis, up from a prior estimate of 4.3%. Weekly initial jobless claims were a lower-than-expected 200,000.

Write to Alexander Osipovich at alexo@wsj.com

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Career as a Power Generation Technician: What To Expect and How To Get Started
data.description:
NORTHAMPTON, MA / ACCESS Newswire / January 22, 2026 / Cummins

If you're interested in ensuring critical systems stay powered and industries keep running, a career as a power generation technician offers hands-on work, problem-solving and the satisfaction of making a real impact. At Cummins we provide training, certifications and career development opportunities to help technicians succeed in this vital field.

What a Day in the Life of a Power Generation Technician Looks Like

Power generation technicians are responsible for maintaining, troubleshooting and repairing generators and power systems in a variety of settings. No two days are the same and the role requires both technical skill and adaptability. A typical day might include:

  • Morning preparation: Reviewing work orders, gathering tools and replacement parts and planning routes to job sites

  • On-site inspections: Arriving at hospitals, data centers or industrial facilities to check generators, engines and electrical systems

  • Troubleshooting and repairs: Diagnosing mechanical or electrical issues using diagnostic software and hands-on techniques then repairing or replacing faulty components

  • System testing: Running load tests, checking backup power systems and ensuring compliance with safety and emissions standards

  • Customer communication: Explaining technical issues and solutions clearly to operators or facility managers to ensure proper system operation

  • Multiple service calls: Traveling between sites to maintain multiple generators or systems in a single day

  • End-of-day reporting: Documenting completed work, noting parts used and updating service logs for the next day

A power generation technician may start the week working on a hospital generator, troubleshoot an industrial backup system midweek and finish with preventive maintenance on a data center system. The variety and responsibility make the role both challenging and rewarding.

Skills Required to Succeed

To excel in this field, technicians need a combination of technical expertise and problem-solving ability:

  • Mechanical and electrical knowledge: Understanding engines, alternators and control systems

  • Diagnostic skills: Quickly identifying and resolving system issues

  • Attention to detail: Ensuring all work meets safety and regulatory standards

  • Communication: Clearly explaining repairs and maintenance plans to clients

  • Adaptability: Managing multiple sites and unexpected equipment challenges

Steps to Becoming a Power Generation Technician

Step 1: Complete High School or GED

Focus on math, physics and technology courses to build a strong foundation.

Step 2: Enroll in a Technical Training Program

Trade schools and community colleges offer programs in engines, electrical systems and power generation. Hands-on labs and fieldwork are essential for real-world readiness.

Step 3: Gain Practical Experience

Internships or apprenticeships provide exposure to on-site troubleshooting and maintenance. Cummins partners with programs to give students valuable field experience.

Step 4: Obtain Certifications

Certifications from ASE, NFPA or manufacturers demonstrate expertise and open career opportunities.

Step 5: Continue Learning

Technology evolves quickly. Training in hybrid systems, renewable integration and advanced diagnostics ensures your skills stay relevant.

Choosing the Right Training Program

When selecting a program, look for:

  • Accreditation from recognized industry organizations

  • Comprehensive curriculum covering mechanical and electrical systems

  • Hands-on training in real-world environments

  • Partnerships with employers like Cummins for field experience

Career Opportunities

Power generation technicians are in demand across healthcare, data centers, industrial facilities and manufacturing. Career growth includes:

  • Specialization in hybrid or renewable systems

  • Leadership roles such as service manager or lead technician

  • Technical trainer or consulting positions

Start Your Power Generation Technician Career

If you're looking for a career with variety, hands-on problem-solving and the satisfaction of supporting critical infrastructure, becoming a power generation technician is an excellent choice. With the right training, certifications and support from Cummins, you'll be prepared to thrive in this essential and rewarding field.

View additional multimedia and more ESG storytelling from Cummins Inc on 3blmedia.com.

Contact Info:

Spokesperson: Cummins Inc

Website: https://www.3blmedia.com/profiles/cummins-inc

Email: info@3blmedia.com

SOURCE: Cummins Inc

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

RNS Number : 0181Q Ming Yang Smart Energy Group Ltd 22 January 2026  

MING YANG SMART ENERGY GROUP LIMITED

(GDR under the symbol: "MYSE")

(a joint stock company established under the laws of the People's Republic of China with limited liability)

Announcement on the Shareholding of the Top Ten Shareholders and Top Ten Tradable Shareholders on the Last Trading Day Before the Suspension of Trading for the Planned Restructuring

Ming Yang Smart Energy Group Limited (hereinafter referred to as the "Company") is planning the issuance of shares and payment of cash for an acquisition of 100% equity in Uniwatt Technology Co., Ltd. together with the raising of supporting funds (hereinafter referred to as the "Transaction"). According to the Administrative Measures for the Major Asset Restructuring of Listed Companies and other relevant regulations, the Transaction is not expected to constitute a major asset restructuring but will constitute a related-party transaction.

In view of the uncertainty of the Transaction, to ensure fair information disclosure, protect the interests of investors, and avoid a significant impact on the Company's stock price, upon the Company's application and in accordance with the relevant regulations of the Shanghai Stock Exchange, the trading of the Company's stock (stock abbreviation: MYSE, stock code: 601615) has been suspended from the opening of the market on January 13, 2026 (Tuesday). The suspension is expected to last for no more than 10 trading days. For details, please refer to the Announcement on Trading Suspension in Respect of the Proposed Issuance of Shares and Cash Payment for Asset Acquisition, Raising of Supporting Funds and Related-Party Transaction disclosed by the Company.

data.title: Elizabeth Hurley tells UK court she endured 'brutal invasion of privacy'
data.description:
Key points:
  • Hurley one of seven claimants suing Daily Mail publisher
  • Actor wipes away tears as she gives evidence
  • Associated Newspapers denies any wrongdoing

By Michael Holden

A tearful Elizabeth Hurley said her landlines and home was bugged as part of a "brutal invasion of privacy" to produce stories, as the actor gave evidence to the High Court in London as part of high-profile privacy lawsuit against the Daily Mail.

Hurley, 60, is one of seven claimants, including Prince Harry and singer Elton John, suing the Mail's publisher Associated Newspapers for alleged privacy violations dating from the early 1990s to the 2010s.

Associated, which also publishes the Mail on Sunday, has called the allegations against it "preposterous smears".

HURLEY WIPES AWAY TEARS

Hurley, the claimants' second witness, is suing over 15 stories which she says featured information obtained unlawfully, including medical details about her pregnancy with son Damian and arguments with his late father, Steve Bing.

While she gave forceful responses to some questions, at times she clutched a tissue to her face and wiped away tears during cross-examination from Associated lawyer Antony White.

Watching on in court was Harry, who appeared in the witness box himself on Wednesday and was sitting next to her son Damian, supportively patting his back when she became emotional.

Hurley rejected suggestions that her friends, including Elton John's husband David Furnish, had passed on information to the press.

She said her phones had been bugged and microphones attached to windows in her house "listening to all my conversations".

"It was deeply hurtful," she said. In her written witness statement, she said her discovery in 2020 about this "brutal invasion of privacy" had left her crushed.

Much of White's questioning centred on whether Hurley could have known about her claims against the Mail sooner, with the publisher arguing that the lawsuits had been filed too late.

She said she first learned about phone hacking from her ex-boyfriend, the actor Hugh Grant, in 2015 when he told her that she could launch a claim against another newspaper group, Mirror Group Newspapers.

She told the court she gave all the damages she won from MGN, 350,000 pounds ($469,770), to 'Hacked Off', a press reform campaign group Grant supports, but said she never really had any "grown-up conversations" with him about the issue.

"Sometimes he tried to tell me but I'm afraid I didn't really listen," she said. "We are just silly together ... maybe I'm not a very good friend."

($1 = 0.7450 pounds)

data.title: Nevada Organic Phosphate Announces DTC Eligibility Allowing for Electronic Settlement of Trades in the United States
data.description:
Vancouver, British Columbia--(Newsfile Corp. - January 22, 2026) - Nevada Organic Phosphate Inc. (OTCQB: NOPFF) ("NOP" or the "Company"), a B.C. based leader in organic sedimentary phosphate exploration, is pleased to announce that its common shares are now eligible for electronic clearing and settlement in the United States through the Depository Trust Company ("DTC").

DTC eligibility is expected to simplify the process of trading and enhance liquidity of NOP shares in the United States. The Company's shares recently began trading on the OTCQB® Venture Market ("OTCQB") under the ticker symbol NOPFF.

DTC is a subsidiary of the Depository Trust & Clearing Corporation, a U.S. company that manages the electronic clearing and settlement of publicly traded companies. Securities that are eligible to be electronically cleared and settled through DTC are considered to be "DTC eligible". This electronic method of clearing securities speeds up the receipt of stock and cash, and thus accelerates the settlement process for investors and brokers, enabling the stock to be traded over a much wider selection of brokerage firms by coming into compliance with their requirements.

Nevada Organic Phosphate Inc. (OTCQB: NOPFF)

The Company is a junior exploration company with an organic sedimentary raw rock phosphate bed, 6.6 kilometres long, in northeast Nevada. Additional applications extend the potential strike of rock phosphate to over 30 kilometres. This is believed to be the only known large-scale organic sedimentary phosphate project in North America. It is situated close to the main highway to Montello/Elko, Nevada, and near the rail head to California.

For More Information

Robin Dow, CEO

T: 604.355.9986

E: robin@dowgroup.ca

data.title: Brazil's tax revenue rises 3.65% to record high in 2025
data.description:
By Marcela Ayres

Brazil's federal tax revenue rose 3.65% in 2025 from the previous year in real terms, the country's tax revenue service said on Thursday, marking a record annual high and helping the government to meet a fiscal budget target despite increased spending.

Tax revenue in December climbed 7.46% in real terms from the same month in 2024, reaching 292.724 billion reais ($55.06 billion), also a record for the period.

The annual performance was driven mainly by higher social security contributions, stronger corporate taxes, and an increase in the IOF financial transactions tax, which the government raised earlier this year on operations including foreign exchange, private pension plans and corporate credit.

President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva's leftist administration is working to meet its fiscal goals under a new 2023 framework that caps spending growth and sets annual primary balance targets.

For 2025, the government is pursuing a zero primary deficit target, with a tolerance range of 0.25% of gross domestic product either way.

Finance Minister Fernando Haddad said the central government ended the year with a 0.1% primary deficit, within the fiscal goal tolerance range, when excluding certain payments that Brazil's fiscal framework allows to be set aside when assessing compliance with the fiscal target.

Official fiscal data will be released by the government later this month.

($1 = 5.3162 reais)
